title: Ensure Team Leads spawn persistent Teammates as persistent, not as transient subagents
description: Observed (Brooks): Team Lead sessions sometimes spawn agents inappropriately — a persistent Teammate gets spawned as a transient Invoked Agent/subagent, losing membership, continuity, and messaging addressability. CONTEXT.md draws the Teammate-vs-Invoked-Agent line by lifecycle, and sprint 4 defect D4-1 already forced a persistent-Teammate-spawning rule mid-sprint, so the failure mode is real here too. Explore how to make correct spawning systematic rather than remembered: candidates include a TEAM_DIRECTIVE/agent-def instruction sweep (roster members spawn via make-teammate/teams, gates spawn via the Agent tool), a remind-only hook on the spawn tool that flags a roster-named agent being spawned transiently (rides the per-feature hook plumbing, ADR-0006), and a doctor/health check that compares the live team roster against the directive roster. Spike first: characterize when and why leads get it wrong, then pick the enforcement. Src-First.
acceptance_criteria: The failure mode is characterized: when a Team Lead spawns a roster Teammate transiently, what in the docs/tooling let it happen
A chosen mechanism (instruction, remind-only hook, or health check) makes the mismatch visible or prevented at spawn time
Spawning a roster-named Teammate persistently and an Invoked Agent transiently both pass without noise
notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Related context: sprint-4 defect D4-1 fix adopted persistent-Teammate spawning for the rest of that sprint.
